rottenhat: It's probably a bit early to play a move on the side like K16.
rottenhat: At the start of the game, the priority is first to play in empty corners, then to approach or enclose from a 3-4 point, then approach or enclose from a 4-4 point
rottenhat: C15 would have been the biggest move for you to play. It's still big for me to enclose the corner, so I will play D14
C[rottenhat: F3 is a big move, but the sequence in top right is not over yet

rottenhat: After you kick at R15 and I extend at Q14, you need to jump to O16 to defend your stones and keep pressure on mine. (You can also play O17, but O16 is usually better because it puts more pressure on my stones)
rottenhat: No matter where on the board this shape appears, you should almost always follow the kick with a jump
rottenhat: If not, your opponent can counterattack your stones
C[rottenhat: Tenuki seems reasonable, since your corner group is safe.
rottenhat: Did you consider making a second approach move in the lower left corner? Since I didn't answer your move at F3, a move on the other side at C6 or D6 would put pressure on my D4 stone
rottenhat: It would be reasonable for me to play C6 now to defend the corner, but I'm going to play H16 to pressure your K16 stone and build my formation in the top left

C[rottenhat: C5 looks a bit strange — it's often better to keep a little further away from stones you want to attack. Because C5 is so close to my D6 stone, I can lean on it to move out and split your stones
C[rottenhat: C6 or D6 would be the normal moves
rottenhat: This maybe is a bit hard to grasp, but the idea here is that you're threatening to seal my stone into the corner with a third move around E5
rottenhat: I have to lean on one of your stones to move out, making the stone I attach to stronger so you gain on that side
rottenhat: So you don't want the other one so close that I can attack it severely afterwards
rottenhat: But here I can give C5 a hard time directly, and the moves you play to defend it will make my group strong enough that I should be able to do something useful against the F3 stone
rottenhat: I'm probably not explaining this very well

C[rottenhat: I guess it would be more helpful if I mentioned other options instead of C3

rottenhat: If I capture C5 straight away, you could play C12 to set up a base and threaten my top left corner or just tenuki to play elsewhere.

rottenhat: When you get a bad result locally, it's tempting to keep playing around that area to try to salvage something, but it's often better just to play elsewhere and see how the board develops — it can give you more options when you come back to that area
